name: Harvest Cornish Hens
recipeIngredient: 4 each 1 3/4 lb. fresh or frozen roasting birds
1 bag Carrots, sliced into 1/4 inch rounds (10-oz)
1 container Brussels sprouts, trimmed and halved (10-oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove giblets and necks from Rock Cornish Hens; refrigerate to use in soup another day.
2. Rinse hens with running cold water; pat dry with paper towels.
3. Sprinkle inside body cavity with salt and pepper.
4. Fold neck skin to back; lift wings toward neck, then fold them under back of hen so they stay in place.
5. With string, tie legs and tail of each hen together.
6. Place hens, breast-side up, on rack in roasting pan.
7. In a cup, mix olive or salad oil, dried parsley flakes, dried thyme leaves, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp pepper; brush hens with oil mixture.
8. Roast hens in 350 degree oven about 1 1/4 hours, brushing hens occasionally with drippings from pan.
9. Hens are done when legs can be moved up and down easily or when fork is inserted between leg and body cavity and juices that escape are not pink.
10. Toward the end of roasting hens, in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat, cook carrots, Brussels sprouts, and 1/2 tsp salt in hot margarine until golden; add 1/2 cup water; over high heat, bring to boiling.
11. Reduce heat to medium-low; cover and cook for 5 to 10 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp, stirring occasionally; keep warm.
12. When hens are done, discard strings.
13. Place hens on a warm large platter; keep warm.
14. For gravy, remove rack from roasting pan; pour drippings into a 1-cup measure; let stand a few seconds until fat separates from meat juice.
15. Skim 2 tbsp fat from drippings into a 3-quart saucepan; skim and discard any remaining fat.
16. In the saucepan over high heat, cook mushrooms until tender and golden, stirring occasionally.
17. Stir enough milk into meat juice to measure 1 cup; stir in flour, chicken bouillon, and 1/2 tsp salt until blended.
18. Add 1/2 cup water to the mushrooms in the saucepan; stir to loosen brown bits from bottom of pan.
19. Pour water and brown bits into the mushrooms in the saucepan.
20. Pour meat-juice mixture into the mushrooms in the saucepan; cook over high heat, stirring constantly until mixture boils and thickens slightly; boil 1 minute.
21. Arrange vegetables on platter with Cornish hens.
22. Garnish with parsley sprigs.
23. Serve with mushroom gravy.
